name: Lord Vishnu
shortDescription: Lord Vishnu is one of the principal deities of Hinduism, revered as the preserver and protector of the universe. He is often depicted with blue skin, holding a conch, discus, lotus, and mace, and is known for his avatars, which descend to Earth to restore dharma.
description: Lord Vishnu is a central deity in Hinduism, forming part of the Trimurti (the Hindu trinity) along with Brahma (the creator) and Shiva (the destroyer). Vishnu's role is that of the preserver and protector of the universe, maintaining cosmic order and dharma (righteousness). He is often depicted with a blue complexion, symbolizing infinity and the cosmic ocean. His four arms typically hold a conch shell (representing the primordial sound of creation), a discus (representing the power of the mind and cosmic order), a lotus flower (representing purity and spiritual liberation), and a mace (representing power and authority). Vishnu resides in Vaikuntha, his celestial abode, with his consort, Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity. A key aspect of Vishnu's mythology is his avatars, or incarnations. These avatars are believed to descend to Earth whenever dharma is threatened by evil. The ten most famous avatars are known as the Dashavatara, including Matsya (fish), Kurma (tortoise), Varaha (boar), Narasimha (half-man/half-lion), Vamana (dwarf), Parashurama (warrior with an axe), Rama (the righteous king of Ayodhya), Krishna (the divine statesman and philosopher), Buddha (the enlightened one), and Kalki (the future avatar who will restore righteousness at the end of the Kali Yuga). The concept of Vishnu and his avatars is deeply intertwined with the cyclical nature of time and the ongoing battle between good and evil. Vishnu's preservation is not merely passive; it involves active intervention to ensure the continuation of cosmic harmony. His worship is widespread throughout India and beyond, and his stories are central to Hindu religious and cultural traditions. The Vishnu Purana and the Bhagavata Purana are important texts dedicated to his life and teachings.
